title: Lactose Tolerance Tests: MedlinePlus Medical Test
text:

During a glucose blood test: 
A health care professional will take a blood sample from a vein in your arm, using a small needle. After the needle is inserted, a small amount of blood will be collected into a test tube or vial. 
You will drink a liquid that contains lactose. 
More blood samples will be taken at specific intervals, usually at 30 minutes, one hour, and two hours. 
Will I need to do anything to prepare for this test? 
To prepare for either type of test, you will need to:
Fast (not eat or drink) for eight to 12 hours before the test 
Stop taking antibiotics Â for two to four weeks before the test 
Not smoke or exercise strenuously the day before the test 
You may also need to stop taking certain medicines before this test, such as laxatives, antacids, and stool softeners, so tell your provider about everything you take. But don't stop taking any medicines unless your provider tells you to.
If you are having a breath test, you may need to brush your teeth or rinse your mouth shortly before the test.
Are there any risks to this test? 
If you are lactose intolerant, you may feel cramps or bloating after drinking the lactose beverage.
There are no other risks to having a hydrogen breath test.
